paragraphs.text: From mudflat surveys at sunrise to classroom visits and grant editing, our volunteers are the lungs of the marsh. Choose a role that fits your schedule, your skills, and your love of the Gulf Coast.
Each program is led by a staff coordinator and pairs new volunteers with experienced mentors. Training is provided; no prior fieldwork experience is required for most roles.
Walk fixed transects through Spartina stands recording vegetation density, erosion markers, and salinity. Data feeds our long-term restoration tracking and informs partner agency reports.
Coordinate teams of 8–20 volunteers on monthly beach and tidal-flat debris removal events. Leads handle safety briefings, waste logging, and end-of-day weigh-ins at the depot.
Visit K–12 classrooms across Nueces and Aransas counties to deliver our 45-minute estuary curriculum. Includes hands-on salinity demos and live brackish-water specimen handling.
Help propagate black mangrove, smooth cordgrass, and Louisiana iris seedlings at our Aransas Pass nursery. Tasks include potting, watering, label maintenance, and pre-planting hardening.
Collect monthly water samples at 14 fixed estuary stations, log temperature, dissolved oxygen, and turbidity, then deliver samples to the TAMU-CC partner lab for analysis.
Conduct point counts and shorebird census walks during spring and fall migrations. Volunteers identify species by sight and call, contributing data to regional flyway monitoring programs.
Assist staff with proofreading, narrative drafting, and budget formatting for federal and foundation grant applications. Most work is remote with weekly check-ins via phone or email.
Repair boardwalks, clear invasive growth, and refresh interpretive signage along our six public access trails. Quarterly weekend workdays include tools, gloves, and lunch on site.
Whether you've got an hour a month or every Saturday free, the path in is the same. New volunteers typically complete onboarding within two to three weeks of first contact.
Tell us which roles appeal to you, your availability, and any relevant experience. The form takes about five minutes to complete.
We'll schedule a short phone or in-person conversation to discuss expectations, match you to a team, and answer any questions about the work.
Complete a half-day general orientation plus any role-specific training (data protocols, field safety, classroom curriculum, or tool handling).
You'll be paired with a mentor for your first two outings. After that, you're a full member of the crew — and the marsh.
Reach out to our volunteer coordinator and we'll match you with a role that fits your schedule and skills. Most volunteers are out in the field within three weeks.
Contact the Conservancy
Questions?
volunteer@brackwatermarsh.org(361) 555-0142, ext. 4
Restoring the Gulf's living edge through community-led science, education, and advocacy since 2009.
1842 Laguna Shore RoadCorpus Christi, TX 78418(361) 555-0142hello@brackwatermarsh.org

visibleText: Brackwater Marsh Conservancy Mission Wetlands Volunteer Grants Contact Get Involved Volunteer With the Conservancy From mudflat surveys at sunrise to classroom visits and grant editing, our volunteers are the lungs of the marsh. Choose a role that fits your schedule, your skills, and your love of the Gulf Coast. Eight Ways to Pitch In Each program is led by a staff coordinator and pairs new volunteers with experienced mentors. Training is provided; no prior fieldwork experience is required for most roles. Field · Year-Round Marsh Monitor Walk fixed transects through Spartina stands recording vegetation density, erosion markers, and salinity. Data feeds our long-term restoration tracking and informs partner agency reports. Time 6 hrs / month Skills Observation, basic GPS Field · Seasonal Shoreline Cleanup Lead Coordinate teams of 8–20 volunteers on monthly beach and tidal-flat debris removal events. Leads handle safety briefings, waste logging, and end-of-day weigh-ins at the depot. Time One Saturday / month Skills Group leadership Education · School Year School Outreach Educator Visit K–12 classrooms across Nueces and Aransas counties to deliver our 45-minute estuary curriculum. Includes hands-on salinity demos and live brackish-water specimen handling. Time 4–8 hrs / month Skills Public speaking Horticulture · Weekly Native Plant Nursery Assistant Help propagate black mangrove, smooth cordgrass, and Louisiana iris seedlings at our Aransas Pass nursery. Tasks include potting, watering, label maintenance, and pre-planting hardening. Time 3 hrs / week Skills Gardening basics Science · Monthly Water Quality Sampler Collect monthly water samples at 14 fixed estuary stations, log temperature, dissolved oxygen, and turbidity, then deliver samples to the TAMU-CC partner lab for analysis. Time 5 hrs / month Skills Careful data entry Science · Spring & Fall Bird Survey Technician Conduct point counts and shorebird census walks during spring and fall migrations. Volunteers identify species by sight and call, contributing data to regional flyway monitoring programs. Time 8 hrs / month seasonal Skills Bird ID, binoculars Remote · Flexible Grant Writing Support Assist staff with proofreading, narrative drafting, and budget formatting for federal and foundation grant applications. Most work is remote with weekly check-ins via phone or email. Time 4 hrs / week Skills Writing, editing Field · Quarterly Trail Maintenance Crew Repair boardwalks, clear invasive growth, and refresh interpretive signage along our six public access trails. Quarterly weekend workdays include tools, gloves, and lunch on site. Time One weekend / quarter Skills Light carpentry helpful How to Join Whether you've got an hour a month or every Saturday free, the path in is the same. New volunteers typically complete onboarding within two to three weeks of first contact. Submit an Interest Form Tell us which roles appeal to you, your availability, and any relevant experience. The form takes about five minutes to complete. Meet a Program Coordinator We'll schedule a short phone or in-person conversation to discuss expectations, match you to a team, and answer any questions about the work. Attend Orientation & Training Complete a half-day general orientation plus any role-specific training (data protocols, field safety, classroom curriculum, or tool handling). Start Your First Shift You'll be paired with a mentor for your first two outings. After that, you're a full member of the crew — and the marsh. Ready to Begin? Reach out to our volunteer coordinator and we'll match you with a role that fits your schedule and skills. Most volunteers are out in the field within three weeks.
